frameworks.name: population receptive field (pRF) modeling
frameworks.one_line: Model-based fit of receptive field position and size from fMRI time series.

frameworks.name: retinotopic mapping
frameworks.one_line: Mapping visual field position to cortical location via spatially modulated stimuli.

core_question: What is the large-scale retinotopic organization of human visual cortex measured with ultrahigh-field 7T fMRI across a large healthy adult cohort?
key_assumptions: pRF position and size are stable summaries of voxel response to visual field stimulation
Time series can be meaningfully averaged across subjects at matched cortical/subcortical locations
manipulations: Spatially modulated visual stimuli for retinotopic mapping
key_findings: Robust pRF signals across occipital, temporal, parietal, frontal cortex and subcortical areas
Group-average results agree with previously published visual area parcellations
Split-half analyses show strong within-subject reliability
interpretations: The dataset and analysis provide reliable estimates of pRF position and size at high spatial resolution across a large cohort
